seo.title: About | Nguyen Van Hai
seo.description: About Nguyen Van Hai — backend engineer focused on scalable architecture, reliability, and practical AI integration.

intro.title: I build backend platforms that help product teams ship faster without sacrificing reliability.
intro.description: I am a backend engineer with 5+ years of experience across LMS, AI-enabled products, and cloud platforms. My focus is not only writing scalable code, but also shaping technical decisions that reduce delivery risk, improve operational confidence, and keep systems maintainable as requirements evolve.

cards.heading: Positioning
cards.body: Backend-first engineer who connects architecture, delivery execution, and product outcomes in one flow.

cards.heading: How I work
cards.body: I align teams on measurable goals early, break delivery into small safe iterations, and document trade-offs so decisions stay clear over time.

cards.heading: Technical strengths
cards.body: Service design, API reliability, performance tuning, and practical AI integration with production-aware constraints.

cards.heading: Delivery principles
cards.body: Bias for clarity, progressive hardening, and operational visibility from day one.

items.label: Architecture with intent
items.text: define boundaries early so features can scale without constant rewrites.

items.label: Execution with ownership
items.text: drive work from design to rollout, including observability and incident feedback loops.

items.label: Collaboration that compounds
items.text: align backend, product, and QA on risk, impact, and release readiness.

items.label: Optimization where it matters
items.text: prioritize bottlenecks that impact user experience, throughput, or team velocity.

seo.title: Projects | Nguyen Van Hai
seo.description: Selected projects by Nguyen Van Hai with role, impact, stack, and measurable delivery outcomes.

intro.title: Selected backend projects where architecture decisions translated into measurable product impact.
intro.description: Each project highlights context, ownership, core contributions, and outcomes that mattered for product and operations.

items.title: Open University Moodle Optimization
items.overview: The platform needed AI-assisted tutoring while keeping student data private and maintaining stable performance under semester-level traffic spikes.

detailLists.heading: Key contributions
items: Built Moodle plugin integrations with an API layer for local LLM orchestration.
Implemented Redis-backed cache strategy for repeated tutor and recommendation flows.
Introduced safer rollout patterns and monitoring for feature reliability.

detailLists.heading: Impact
items: Reduced database load by ~40% on heavy query paths.
Improved perceived response time for AI tutor interactions.
Enabled privacy-conscious deployment by running models on internal infrastructure.

items.title: Microleap AI Food Detector Platform
items.overview: The product required near real-time food recognition with stable processing throughput and consistent releases across multiple environments.

detailLists.heading: Key contributions
items: Developed event-driven Golang services for image processing and metadata workflows.
Integrated vector search using Qdrant to improve similarity matching quality.
Standardized containerized deployment with Kubernetes-first operational patterns.

detailLists.heading: Impact
items: Improved release confidence through repeatable CI/CD and environment parity.
Scaled processing capacity with clearer service isolation and queue-driven execution.
Reduced production incidents by tightening contracts between backend components.

items.title: LMS API Reliability & Performance Program
items.overview: Growing LMS adoption created API bottlenecks and operational friction during peak usage periods, requiring a structured reliability push.

detailLists.heading: Key contributions
items: Refactored high-traffic endpoints and optimized SQL/query plans for critical paths.
Introduced performance baselines and observability dashboards for release gates.
Codified incident learnings into repeatable runbooks and delivery checklists.

detailLists.heading: Impact
items: Improved key API response times by approximately 40%.
Raised deployment stability with clearer quality gates and rollback readiness.
Reduced firefighting by increasing visibility into latency and failure trends.
